title: Intense Bitter Malaysian Steak Satay
description: A pescatarian-friendly Malaysian dish featuring skirty steak, sashimi-cut, with a bitter and pillowy texture, this crowd-pleaser is sure to impress. It combines the richness of steak with the brightness of citrus and the depth of bitter flavors.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender or food processor, combine lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves, and blend until well combined.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, and palm sugar mixture.
Add the bitter melon to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Add the skirty steak to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade, making sure each piece is well coated.
Thread the marinated steak and bitter melon onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the satay for 4-5 minutes per side, or until the steak is cooked to an internal temperature of 130 degrees Fahrenheit.
Let the satay rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the satay hot, garnished with additional kaffir lime leaves and lemongrass if desired.
